name: Actrapid Insulin Infusion

preparations.label: Standard Preparation
preparationSteps: Take 50 IU of insulin (0.5 mls from 100 IU/ml vial or pen)
Dilute with 49.5 ml of Normal Saline = 50 mls dilution
preparations.notes: Ref: Clinical Practice Guidelines on Management of Type 2 DM, KKM 2015

administrationGuidance: Commence Fixed Rate IV Insulin Infusion. Aim to drop Blood Glucose Level (BGL)/ Glucometer (GM) 2-5 mmol/L per HOUR. INCREASE if BGL doesn't drop by a minimum of 2 mmol/hr. INCREASE if serum ketone doesn't decrease by minimum of 0.5mmol/hr. REDUCE dose if BGL dropping too rapidly > 5 mmol/hr. Calculator support is limited as titration is based on BGL, not purely weight.
importantCautions: Titrate according to Blood Glucose Levels.
references: Clinical Practice Guidelines on Management of Type 2 DM, KKM 2015
Hospital Sultanah Aminah Guidelines for Drug Reconstitution, 2013
